title: rcicrely
text: R package reliability assessment classification images (CIs) produced reverse correlation experiments. Covers within-condition consistency (permuted split-half Spearman-Brown, leave-one-, ICC) -condition discriminability (cluster-based permutation testing, representational dissimilarity). Works standard 2IFC (via canonical rcicr, Dotsch v1.0.1) Brief-RC (Schmitz, Rougier, & Yzerbyt, 2024 — owned natively package). Companion package rcicrdiagnostics. Diagnostics catches silent data-processing errors CI computation; rcicrely asks whether CIs reliable discriminable cleanly computed. Independent: neither imports . file design reference rcicrely. complete enough competent R developer — AI assistant — can rebuild package scratch using nothing document. Every non-obvious implementation choice, every rcicr integration gotcha cost real time companion rcicrdiagnostics build, every statistical commitment captured . text: rel_split_half(signal_matrix, n_permutations = 2000, seed = NULL). permutation: randomly partition N participants two roughly equal halves. odd N, one participant randomly dropped per permutation (participant every time — re-drawn iteration) halves contain floor(N/2) participants. Document help page. Average half’s columns get two group-level signal vectors. Compute Pearson correlation. Mean r_hh = average per-permutation r. Spearman-Brown correction: r_SB = (2 × r_hh) / (1 + r_hh). 95% CI: 2.5th 97.5th percentile permutation distribution (percentile method). Separately r r_SB. Note: permutation participants, trials. Participant halves averaged correlation — averaging matters mathematically standard approach psychometrics literature.

what: 7.3. ICC(3,1) and ICC(3,k) from mean squares
title: rcicrely
text: rel_icc(signal_matrix, variants = c("3_1", "3_k")). ICC differs ICCs RC literature. ICCs published reverse-correlation papers trait-rating reliability statistics: phase-2 naive raters score group-level individual CIs trait dimensions (trustworthy, competent, dominant) ICC reports inter-rater agreement Likert scores. design exactly two-phase pipeline Cone et al. (2020) criticised — reported reliability inherits biases introduced rating phase (rater pool, scale anchors, contextual framing). rcicrely’s ICC different statistical object. operates directly pixel-level signal produced original producers, sidesteps phase-2 rating entirely. Treat feature, deviation: whole package quantifying reliability without relying downstream raters. **Model choice — ICC(3,*) positive argument, convention.** Map signal matrix: pixels = targets, participants = raters. : **ICC(1,*) (one-way random, crossed design): rejected.** design crossed — every participant’s signal evaluated every pixel. Using ICC(1,*) ignores crossed variance structure. **ICC(2,*) (two-way random; pixels raters random): rejected.** Pixels fixed img_size × img_size grid, random sample population pixels. Users generalising “pixels might measured”; using every pixel image. random-targets assumption category error, even ICC(2,) ICC(3,) happen give similar numbers (see note ). ICC(3,1), ICC(3,k) (two-way mixed; pixels fixed, participants random): correct specification. Participants random sample plausible-producer population. Pixels fixed experimental image. ship ICC(3,1) ICC(3,k). answer different questions: ICC(3,1) — reliability single participant’s CI signal estimate group-level pattern. Answers informative one individual CI? ICC(3,k) — reliability group-mean CI across k participants. Answers stable group CI experiment produced? usually headline number paper. Numerical proximity caveat. n_pixels ≈ 262,144 n_participants ≈ 30, ICC(2,) ICC(3,) typically differ < 0.01 pixel rater main-effect variance components tiny relative interaction term. model choice still matters interpretation: ICC(3,) reports defensible population-generalisation statement; ICC(2,) reports nonsensical one happens give similar number. Compatibility option. variants permits "2_1" "2_k" users whose reviewers explicitly ask ICC(2,*) form. Default c("3_1", "3_k") print method states shipped model. Implementation — direct mean squares, psych::ICC(). Non-negotiable: psych::ICC() 262,144 × 30 matrix allocates intermediate blows memory. Return shape: $icc_3_1, $icc_3_k, $ms_rows, $ms_cols, $ms_error, $n_raters, $n_targets, $model (character scalar: e.g. "ICC(3,*) / two-way mixed; pixels fixed, participants random"). variants includes "2_1" "2_k", add $icc_2_1 / $icc_2_k. References. Shrout & Fleiss (1979) original six-ICC taxonomy; McGraw & Wong (1996) modern re-derivation random/fixed-effects clarification. Cite @references rel_icc(). Test psych::ICC() small-matrix case (≤ 50 × 10) development — numbers must match 6 decimals.
code: # signal_matrix is pixels (targets) × participants (raters) n <- nrow(signal_matrix)   # n targets (pixels) — fixed k <- ncol(signal_matrix)   # k raters (participants) — random  row_means  <- rowMeans(signal_matrix) col_means  <- colMeans(signal_matrix) grand_mean <- mean(signal_matrix)  ss_rows  <- k * sum((row_means - grand_mean)^2) ss_cols  <- n * sum((col_means - grand_mean)^2) ss_total <- sum((signal_matrix - grand_mean)^2) ss_error <- ss_total - ss_rows - ss_cols  ms_rows  <- ss_rows  / (n - 1) ms_cols  <- ss_cols  / (k - 1) ms_error <- ss_error / ((n - 1) * (k - 1))  # ICC(3,1), ICC(3,k) — two-way mixed, pixels fixed, raters random icc_3_1  <- (ms_rows - ms_error) /             (ms_rows + (k - 1) * ms_error)  icc_3_k  <- (ms_rows - ms_error) / ms_rows  # ICC(2,1), ICC(2,k) — exposed only via variants = c("2_1", "2_k") icc_2_1  <- (ms_rows - ms_error) /             (ms_rows + (k - 1) * ms_error +              (k / n) * (ms_cols - ms_error))  icc_2_k  <- (ms_rows - ms_error) /             (ms_rows + (ms_cols - ms_error) / n)

title: rcicrely
text: rel_cluster_test(signal_matrix_a, signal_matrix_b, img_dims, ...). Implementation: Compute observed pixel-wise Welch t (§7.4). Build observed cluster map: threshold |t| > cluster_threshold (default 2.0), separately positive negative clusters. 2.0 default approximates p < 0.05 large df, small samples (N_A + N_B < 20) -rejects; users inspect observed-t histogram trusting result, consider raising threshold increasing N. Find connected components 4-connectivity (8-connectivity — conservative choice; document ). cluster, cluster mass = sum t-values within cluster (count pixels). captures extent magnitude. Null distribution: n_permutations permutations, randomly shuffle condition labels across participants (pixels — never pixels; preserving spatial correlation participant’s CI point test). Permutation stratified: every permutation preserves (N_A, N_B) exactly. Unstratified permutation let two groups’ sizes drift, changing Welch degrees freedom null biasing max-mass distribution. Recompute pixel-wise Welch t shuffled labels, find clusters, record maximum positive maximum negative cluster mass. observed cluster: p-value fraction permutation null-distribution masses (matching direction) exceed mass. Maximum-statistic approach gives FWER control. Reference: Maris & Oostenveld (2007) cluster-based permutation approach; Nichols & Holmes (2002) max-stat FWER. Implementation notes: Connected component labeling: BFS thresholded binary matrix. Pure R, compiled code. Pre-allocate label matrix queue integer vectors head/tail pointers. Process positive negative masks separately. Memory management: never copy full signal matrices inside permutation loop — permute integer column indices recompute t shuffled view.

title: rcicrely
text: concrete choice. paper deviates one, update code section; drift silently. Signal extraction: CI_signal = CI_pixels - base_image_pixels. reliability computations operate signal, never raw CI pixels (include shared base image inflate correlations). Caveat (raw vs rendered, v0.1.1): CI_pixels comes rendered PNG (Mode 1), base + scaling(mask), CI_signal scaling(mask), raw mask. Variance-based reliability metrics (ICC, Euclidean dissimilarity, pixel t, cluster mass) sensitive; Pearson-based metrics survive single uniform linear scaling, even break per-CI “matched” scaling. canonical 2IFC rcicr::computeInfoVal2IFC() unaffected — source norm(matrix(target_ci[["ci"]]), "f"), always reading raw $ci rcicr CI-list. hand-rolled infoVal implementations (Brief-RC; custom 2IFC code re-implements norm) need raw mask explicitly. Mode 2 (ci_from_responses_*) returns raw mask directly. package emits one-time per-session warning Mode-1 input boundary heuristic backstop every rel_* entry. Brief-RC mask: Schmitz’s genMask() exactly — mean(response) per unique chosen stim, denominator = number unique chosen pool ids (raw trial count). See §5.2 duplicate-stimulus tie-breaking rule. Schmitz et al. (2024) §3.1.2. Split-half reliability: participants permuted; halves averaged correlation; odd N one participant randomly dropped per permutation (re-drawn iteration). Spearman-Brown correction r_SB = (2 r_hh) / (1 + r_hh). Brinkman et al. (2017) RC-specific practice; Shrout & Fleiss (1979) psychometric foundation. ICC: ICC(3,1) ICC(3,k) via mean-squares — two-way mixed model, pixels fixed, participants random. ICC(2,*) (require pixels random sample pixel population, ) psych::ICC() (memory). See §7.3 full model-selection argument. Shrout & Fleiss (1979); McGraw & Wong (1996). Pixel-wise t: Welch’s (unequal variances), Student’s. Cluster test permutation: condition labels, across participants, stratified — every permutation preserves (N_A, N_B). Never pixel labels. Cluster connectivity: 4-connectivity (conservative 8-connectivity). Document choice help pages rel_cluster_test() run_between() (two exposures). Cluster mass: sum t-values within cluster, pixel count. FWER control: max-statistic across permutations. Maris & Oostenveld (2007); Nichols & Holmes (2002). Bootstrap CIs: percentile method (2.5th / 97.5th) via base R quantile(). boot dependency. BCa future work. doubt, test implementation synthetic data known ground truth trusting output real data.

title: rcicrely
text: Design (canonical spec — keep one version, don’t drift): 30 producers, contributing sessions target- pattern conditions (vs B, different latent signals — e.g. emphasises eyes, B emphasises mouth). Total sessions = 30 × 2 = 60. Producers split three trial-count levels (300 / 500 / 1000 trials per session), 10 producers per level. 10 producers runs trial count B. Within (trial-count × condition) cell 10 producers, archetype mix 7 good / 2 weak / 1 random. “good signal”: moderate bias toward condition’s target latent pattern, SNR ≈ 0.3. “weak signal”: lower SNR (≈ 0.1); borderline metrics expected. “random”: coin-flip responses; noise . Expected within-condition results (trial count ≈ 500, per condition): split-half r ≈ 0.4–0.6; LOO without dramatic outliers default 2.5-SD threshold; ICC(3,1) ≈ 0.1–0.3; ICC(3,k) well 0.5 (averaging boosts reliability). Expected -condition results: rel_cluster_test() finds significant clusters face regions differ two target patterns. rel_dissimilarity() returns moderate Euclidean distance moderate positive correlation.

what: 16. Lessons learned from the companion package
title: rcicrely
text: Distilled rcicrdiagnostics build — every one cost real time: PNG-derived signals scaled, raw. read_cis() reads rendered disk: base + scaling(mask). extract_signal() therefore yields scaling(mask), raw mask. Variance-based reliability metrics (ICC, Euclidean dissimilarity, t-tests, cluster mass) wrong . Pearson-based metrics survive single uniform scaling break per-CI “matched”. rcicr::computeInfoVal2IFC() affected — verified rcicr v1.0.1 source: norm(matrix(target_ci[["ci"]]), "f"), always pulling raw $ci CI-list element. infoVal pitfall applies hand-rolled implementations (Brief-RC, custom code). Mode 2 (ci_from_responses_*) safe path everything. Lesson realised mid-v0.1.1; remedy release -per-session warning input boundary plus looks_scaled() heuristic backstop wired every rel_* / run_* entry. Documentation chapter 3 user-guide vignette single source truth users pointed . Don’t invent data formats. earlier rcicrdiagnostics attempt used “expanded” Brief-RC format (12 rows per trial, weight 1 chosen / -1/11 unchosen). ’s Schmitz. Users got confused -0.0909… values. Read paper. Match paper. per-trial signed-selection format Schmitz et al. §3.1.2 describes Schmitz’s genMask() expects. Don’t guess statistics. rcicrdiagnostics skips Brief-RC infoVal rather ship subtly wrong reference distribution. rcicrely implements statistics can audit — anything beyond gets implemented can validated synthetic ground truth. Don’t hallucinate authors citations. extended author list Brinkman et al. (2019) using names “remembered”. Three six wrong. Use citations exactly supplied author, verify DOI. Don’t trust external-package READMEs branch names. rcicr’s @development branch doesn’t exist; older README said . git ls-remote authoritative. Rename aggressively pre-1.0. rcicrdiagnostics run_all_checks() became obvious checks run default (rest gated optional inputs). Renamed run_diagnostics(). rcicrely, consider upfront whether name oversells behaviour — fix users depend . Rcicr v1.0.1 authoritative rcicr. Canonical. 17 functions. Brief-RC. Anything else someone’s fork. Verify identity installed rcicr packageVersion("rcicr"), packageDescription("rcicr")$Author, length(ls("package:rcicr")). PSOCK clusters macOS racy. ncores = 1L safe default. Case matters filesystems thought case-insensitive. macOS find -name "*.RData" case-sensitive. ignore.case = TRUE always. Vignette-eval chunks load installed package. function rename, reinstall re-rendering, rmarkdown::render() executes stale code. Temp folders pollute R CMD check. drop third-party source tree temp/ reference, .Rbuildignore (^temp$) .gitignore .

title: rcicrely
text: Independent packages, mutual imports. Shared design: two-pipeline model (2IFC + Brief-RC). data.table + cli infrastructure. S3-result discipline: every exported function returns object consistent shape, dedicated print/summary/plot methods. Shared bogus data convention: packages’ data generators use column names (participant_id, trial, stimulus, response, rt), archetype layout (20 signal / 6 biased / 2 constant / 2 inverted rcicrdiagnostics’ pattern; rcicrely’s slightly different needs signal/noise differentiation across conditions). set.seed() values reproducibility across packages matters cross-referenced vignettes. Intended workflow: User runs rcicrdiagnostics::run_diagnostics() raw response data catch coding errors, response bias, alignment bugs. Fix anything fails. User generates CIs (via rcicr::generateCI2IFC() 2IFC rcicrely’s native function Brief-RC). User runs rcicrely::run_within() condition check within-condition reliability. User runs rcicrely::run_between() pairs conditions test discriminability. Eventually, rcicrely clean Brief-RC infoVal implementation, rcicrdiagnostics’ compute_infoval_summary(method = "briefrc") can delegate , lifting current "skip" status.

Tests: 15 test-*.R files tests/testthat/. 116 passing, 1 skipped (psych cross-check, gated skip_if_not_installed("psych")). R CMD check ---cran: 0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 notes (0/0/1 benign “unable verify current time” note offline runs). Vignette: vignettes/tutorial.Rmd renders ~220 KB HTML. 8 chapters: package / isn’t, install, data modes, within-condition, -condition, Brief-RC end--end, interpretation, references. pkgdown: _pkgdown.yml organises reference pages function area. pkgdown::build_site() produces site 14 reference pages + tutorial article. yet deployed — push toggle repo settings (§14.3) activate. See temp/README.md run order.

title: rcicrely user guide
text: reverse correlation (RC), participants’ trial-level choices project onto pool noise patterns reconstruct mental representation used. output per-producer classification image (CI) plus group-level CI obtained averaging. infoVal (Brinkman et al., 2019) tells whether CI contains signal chance. tell whether signal stable across producers, replicable different subset producers, distinguishable another condition’s CI. rcicrely fills gap pixel-level reliability toolkit: Within-condition (consistent condition’s CI across producers?): permuted split-half Spearman-Brown, leave-one-sensitivity, ICC(3,1) ICC(3,k). -condition (condition ’s CI distinguishable condition B’s?): vectorised Welch t per pixel, cluster-based permutation max-statistic FWER, bootstrap representational dissimilarity. Critically, none requires phase-2 trait-rating study. metrics read directly pixel-level signal produced original producers, sidestepping two-phase design Cone et al. (2020) flagged.

title: rcicrely user guide
text: Mode 2 returns raw mask; Mode 1 , PNGs disk always rendered. Prefer Mode 2 raw responses available. must use Mode 1, package emits one-time warning per session reminding caveat. silence: generated PNGs rcicr::generateCI2IFC(scaling = "none"), rendered output effectively raw, warning genuinely false positive.
code: Mode 1: PNGs on disk           Mode 2: raw responses           |                              |    read_cis()                    ci_from_responses_2ifc() / _briefrc()           |                              |    extract_signal()                      |           |                              |    scaling(mask)                       mask                    <-- raw           |                              |           +---------> rel_*() <----------+ # acknowledge per-call: signal <- load_signal_matrix("data/cis/", "data/base.jpg",                              acknowledge_scaling = TRUE)  # or silence for the whole session: options(rcicrely.silence_scaling_warning = TRUE)

title: rcicrely user guide
text: scaling underlying mask. Pearson r per column exactly 1 (linear scaling correlation- preserving), row-mean variance ICC formula depends ~25x larger rendered version. ICC(3,*) computed rendered data therefore ICC computed raw mask. logic applies Euclidean distance infoVal.
code: set.seed(1) n_pix <- 32L * 32L n_p   <- 20L mask  <- matrix(rnorm(n_pix * n_p, sd = 0.02), n_pix, n_p)  # Render: stretch each column to [-0.5, 0.5] (Schmitz "matched") rendered <- apply(mask, 2L, function(m) {   rng <- diff(range(m)); if (rng == 0) m else m * (1 / rng) })  c(   raw_max_range      = round(diff(range(mask)),     3L),   rendered_max_range = round(diff(range(rendered)), 3L) ) #>      raw_max_range rendered_max_range  #>              0.162              1.159  # Pearson r between same-column raw and rendered: ~ 1 # (a perfect linear stretch preserves Pearson) mean(vapply(seq_len(n_p),             function(j) cor(mask[, j], rendered[, j]),             numeric(1L))) #> [1] 1  # But the variance ratios that drive ICC are very different var(rowMeans(mask))      # variance of the group mean over pixels #> [1] 1.923774e-05 var(rowMeans(rendered))  # same metric, on the rendered version #> [1] 0.001134698

title: rcicrely user guide
text: . Reads every PNG/JPEG directory, converts grayscale, returns pixels participants numeric matrix. use . one CI image per producer already saved disk want load R numeric matrix. Pair extract_signal() get base-subtracted signal matrix. Reading result. nrow = n_pixels, ncol = n_files. img_dims attribute carries c(nrow, ncol) original image plotting helpers know reshape. Common mistakes. Forgetting subtract base passing rel_* function (use extract_signal() convenience wrapper load_signal_matrix()). Mixing CIs different sizes - aborts loudly. Trusting reliability numbers without reading raw vs. rendered note (chapter 3).
code: # Make a tiny synthetic dataset on disk to demonstrate against. demo_dir <- tempfile("ci_demo_"); dir.create(demo_dir) base_img <- matrix(0.5, 32L, 32L) png_path <- function(i) file.path(demo_dir, sprintf("p%02d.png", i)) if (requireNamespace("png", quietly = TRUE)) {   for (i in 1:6) {     img <- pmin(pmax(base_img + matrix(rnorm(32 * 32, sd = 0.05), 32, 32),                      0), 1)     png::writePNG(img, png_path(i))   } } cis <- read_cis(demo_dir, acknowledge_scaling = TRUE) dim(cis) #> [1] 1024    6 attr(cis, "img_dims") #> [1] 32 32

title: rcicrely user guide

title: rcicrely user guide
text: rest guide ’ll work small synthetic dataset two conditions different latent signals.
code: set.seed(1) make_condition <- function(region_top, n_pix = 32L * 32L,                            n_p = 30L, snr = 0.3) {   noise <- matrix(rnorm(n_pix * n_p, sd = 0.05), n_pix, n_p)   mask <- if (region_top) {     c(rep(1, n_pix / 2L), rep(0, n_pix / 2L))   } else {     c(rep(0, n_pix / 2L), rep(1, n_pix / 2L))   }   out <- noise + snr * outer(mask, runif(n_p, 0.7, 1.3))   attr(out, "img_dims") <- c(32L, 32L)   out } sig_A <- make_condition(region_top = TRUE,  n_p = 30L) sig_B <- make_condition(region_top = FALSE, n_p = 30L)

title: rcicrely user guide
text: . Estimates stable condition’s group CI across random halves producers. Reports per-permutation correlation halves Spearman-Brown projection full sample. use . want answer “get group CI ’d run different half participants?”. r_SB usually headline number. Permutation distribution split-half r. Reading result. $r_hh = mean per-split r. $r_sb = Spearman-Brown projected reliability full sample (report unless asked otherwise). $ci_95, $ci_95_sb percentile CIs. $distribution full per-permutation r vector. Common mistakes. Reporting $r_hh instead $r_sb audience cares full-sample CI. Running n_permutations < 200 trusting CIs.
code: r_sh <- rel_split_half(sig_A, n_permutations = 500L, seed = 1L,                        progress = FALSE) print(r_sh) #> <rcicrely split-half reliability> #>   N producers:          30 #>   n_permutations:       500 #>   mean per-split r:     0.993  [0.992, 0.993] #>   Spearman-Brown r_SB:  0.996  [0.996, 0.997] plot(r_sh)

title: rcicrely user guide
text: . producer, correlates full-sample group CI group CI computed without producer. Producers unusually low LOO correlation flagged. use . Spotting producers whose CI sits far group pattern. default mean +/- SD; use flag_method = "mad" median +/- MAD rule doesn’t get pulled around outliers tries detect: Per-producer LOO correlation. Reading result. $correlations per-producer vector. $flagged lists ids threshold. $mean_r / $sd_r / $median_r / $mad_r centre/spread rules can compare. $flag_method, $flag_threshold record used. Common mistakes. Treating $flagged “drop producers” - investigate first (Cone et al. 2020 caveat: flagged producer may genuinely atypical mental representation). Cross-check rcicrdiagnostics rule response-coding errors.
code: r_loo <- rel_loo(sig_A, flag_threshold = 2.5, flag_method = "sd") print(r_loo) #> <rcicrely leave-one-out sensitivity> #>   N producers:        30 #>   mean LOO r:         1.000 #>   SD:                 0.000 #>   median LOO r:       1.000 #>   MAD:                0.000 #>   flag rule:          r < mean - 2.50 * SD   (=> r < 1.000) #>   flagged producers:  none r_loo_mad <- rel_loo(sig_A, flag_threshold = 2.5, flag_method = "mad") c(sd_threshold = r_loo$threshold, mad_threshold = r_loo_mad$threshold) #>  sd_threshold mad_threshold  #>     0.9999303     0.9999308 plot(r_loo)

title: rcicrely user guide
text: . Two-way mixed ICC via direct mean squares. Default returns ICC(3,1) ICC(3,k). Pixels fixed (image grid), participants random (drawn producer population). use . Single-scalar reliability summary. ICC(3,k) “stable group-mean CI?”, ICC(3,1) “informative one producer’s CI?”. also report ICC(2,*) reviewer expects : Reading result. $icc_3_1, $icc_3_k, $icc_2_1, $icc_2_k (latter two NA unless requested). $ms_rows, $ms_cols, $ms_error underlying ANOVA mean squares transparency. $model description string print method shows. Common mistakes. Asking ICC(2,) reviewer expects . ICC(2,) treats pixels random sample pixel population, aren’t. numbers usually close ICC(3,*) high pixel counts model mis-specified - report variants = c("3_1", "3_k", "2_1", "2_k") asked. ICC strongly scale-sensitive. Use raw mask .
code: r_icc <- rel_icc(sig_A, variants = c("3_1", "3_k")) print(r_icc) #> <rcicrely ICC> #>   model:        ICC(3,*) / two-way mixed; pixels fixed, participants random #>   N targets:    1024 pixels #>   N raters:     30 participants #>  #>   ICC(3,1):      0.8788 #>   ICC(3,k):      0.9954 r_icc_full <- rel_icc(sig_A, variants = c("3_1", "3_k", "2_1", "2_k")) print(r_icc_full) #> <rcicrely ICC> #>   model:        ICC(3,*) / two-way mixed; pixels fixed, participants random #>   N targets:    1024 pixels #>   N raters:     30 participants #>  #>   ICC(3,1):      0.8788 #>   ICC(3,k):      0.9954 #>   ICC(2,1):      0.8565 #>   ICC(2,k):      0.9944

code: within_A <- run_within(   sig_A,   n_permutations = 500L,   seed           = 1L,   progress       = FALSE ) print(within_A) #> <rcicrely report: within> #> --- #> * $results$split_half #> <rcicrely split-half reliability> #>   N producers:          30 #>   n_permutations:       500 #>   mean per-split r:     0.993  [0.992, 0.993] #>   Spearman-Brown r_SB:  0.996  [0.996, 0.997] #> --- #> * $results$loo #> <rcicrely leave-one-out sensitivity> #>   N producers:        30 #>   mean LOO r:         1.000 #>   SD:                 0.000 #>   median LOO r:       1.000 #>   MAD:                0.000 #>   flag rule:          r < mean - 2.50 * SD   (=> r < 1.000) #>   flagged producers:  none #> --- #> * $results$icc #> <rcicrely ICC> #>   model:        ICC(3,*) / two-way mixed; pixels fixed, participants random #>   N targets:    1024 pixels #>   N raters:     30 participants #>  #>   ICC(3,1):      0.8788 #>   ICC(3,k):      0.9954

title: rcicrely user guide
text: . Vectorised Welch’s t per pixel two condition signal matrices. use . want raw t-map (e.g. custom plotting, threshold sensitivity analysis) without cluster-level inference. Welch’s t (unequal variances) correct - 2IFC Brief-RC conditions can differ N variance.
code: t_vec <- pixel_t_test(sig_A, sig_B) range(t_vec); length(t_vec) #> [1] -25.20620  27.27447 #> [1] 1024

title: rcicrely user guide
text: . Cluster-based permutation test max-statistic FWER control. Pixels threshold form clusters via 4-connectivity; cluster mass = sum t-values inside; null built stratified label permutation; max-mass approach controls family-wise error. use . “image two conditions differ?” proper multiple-comparison control across pixels. Per-pixel Welch t-map; significant clusters outlined. Reading result. $observed_t raw t-map. $clusters data.frame cluster_id, direction, mass, size, p_value, significant. $pos_labels / $neg_labels integer matrices plotting overlays. $null_distribution exposes per-permutation max masses. Common mistakes. Reading cluster_threshold = 2.0 “p < 0.05” - t-cutoff forming candidate clusters, cluster significance threshold. Significance cluster’s mass relative null. Trusting cluster p-values n_permutations < 1000.
code: clust <- rel_cluster_test(   sig_A, sig_B,   img_dims          = c(32L, 32L),   n_permutations    = 500L,   cluster_threshold = 2.0,   alpha             = 0.05,   seed              = 1L,   progress          = FALSE ) print(clust) #> <rcicrely cluster-based permutation test> #>   N_A = 30, N_B = 30 #>   image dims:        32 x 32 #>   cluster threshold: |t| > 2.00 #>   n_permutations:    500 #>   alpha:             0.05 #>   clusters found:    2 (2 significant) #>  #>  cluster_id direction  mass size p_value significant #>           1       neg -9886  512       0        TRUE #>           1       pos  9807  512       0        TRUE plot(clust)

title: rcicrely user guide
text: . Two scalar dissimilarity metrics two conditions’ group CIs - Pearson correlation Euclidean distance - percentile bootstrap CIs. use . “different two conditions overall?” uncertainty. Pair rel_cluster_test() spatial information. Bootstrap distributions Pearson r Euclidean distance. Reading result. $correlation, $euclidean observed values; $ci_cor, $ci_dist percentile CIs; $boot_cor, $boot_dist full bootstrap distributions. Common mistakes. Reading $correlation near 1 “conditions look ” - identical spatial patterns different magnitudes give r ~ 1; magnitude difference shows $euclidean. $euclidean scales sqrt(n_pixels) within-study metric, comparable across resolutions.
code: diss <- rel_dissimilarity(   sig_A, sig_B,   n_boot   = 500L,   ci_level = 0.95,   seed     = 1L,   progress = FALSE ) print(diss) #> <rcicrely representational dissimilarity> #>   n_boot:     500 #>   CI level:   95% #>   Pearson r   = -0.996   [-0.994, -0.991]   SE = 0.001 #>   Euclidean   = 9.668   [9.293, 10.089]   SE = 0.207 plot(diss)

title: rcicrely user guide
text: Schmitz, Rougier & Yzerbyt (2024) introduced Brief-RC 12: trial shows 12 noisy faces (6 oriented, 6 inverted, drawn shared noise pool); participant picks one. recorded format one row per trial, columns participant_id, trial, stimulus (chosen pool id), response (+1 oriented, -1 inverted). rcicrely’s implementation native (rcicr _brief functions, don’t exist canonical rcicr v1.0.1). Brief-RC infoVal deferred (canonical implementation either rcicr rcicrely). needs reference distribution matched producer’s trial count, pool size. rel_* reliability metrics depend . hand-roll infoVal Brief-RC today, compute raw mask (res$signal_matrix), res$rendered_ci saved PNG – carry scaling step give wrong reference comparison.
code: # 1. Generate the noise pool (once per project) - heavy step. rcicr::generateStimuli2IFC(   base_face_files     = list(base = "data/base.jpg"),   n_trials            = 600L,        # becomes the Brief-RC pool size   img_size            = 256L,   stimulus_path       = "data/stim",   label               = "mystudy",   ncores              = 1L,          # macOS-safe default   return_as_dataframe = TRUE,        # capture the noise matrix!   seed                = 1L ) |> saveRDS("data/noise_matrix.rds")  # cache for fast loading  # 2. Collect Brief-RC responses in the per-trial format.  # 3. Compute individual CIs. res <- ci_from_responses_briefrc(   responses       = my_brief_rc_responses,   noise_matrix    = readRDS("data/noise_matrix.rds"),   base_image_path = "data/base.jpg",   method          = "briefrc12",   scaling         = "matched"   # also rendered for plotting )  # 4. Reliability assessment. within  <- run_within(res$signal_matrix, seed = 1L) between <- run_between(res_A$signal_matrix, res_B$signal_matrix,                        seed = 1L)  # 5. Save rendered CI to PNG (visualization only - DO NOT use for stats) if (requireNamespace("png", quietly = TRUE)) {   for (j in seq_along(res$participants)) {     img <- matrix(res$rendered_ci[, j], res$img_dims[1], res$img_dims[2])     img <- pmin(pmax(img, 0), 1)     png::writePNG(img, sprintf("plots/p%02d.png", j))   } }

title: rcicrely user guide
text: Sample size. rcicrely aborts 4 producers per condition warns 30 (Cone et al. 2020 recommend N >= 60 stable reliability assessment). N = 10 metrics computable CIs wide. Split-half. Think r_hh ~ 0.3 weak, 0.5 moderate, 0.7+ strong producer-level consistency. r_SB report asked full-sample CI. LOO flagging. flag starting point investigation, verdict. Use flag_method = "mad" SD rule dominated outliers tries find. ICC(3,) vs ICC(2,). Numerically close high pixel counts ICC(3,*) correctly-specified model. Don’t swap reviewer; report variants = c("3_1", "3_k", "2_1", "2_k") pressed. Cluster threshold != p-value. cluster_threshold = 2.0 t-cutoff forming clusters. Significance decided mass relative null. default roughly two-tailed p < 0.05 large df, small N -rejects - inspect t histogram. Raw vs. rendered CIs. package’s reliability math correct given raw signal matrix. Mode 1 (PNGs disk) loads scaling(mask) instead mask; results differ raw responses produce. size difference depends scaling applied PNG-write time. Mode 2 safe path. infoVal correctness. rcicr::computeInfoVal2IFC() extracts raw $ci input CI-list internally, canonical 2IFC path safe regardless scaling. pitfall hand-rolled infoVal implementations – Brief-RC, custom code consumes bare matrix – must fed raw mask. Feeding rendered/scaled values silently distorts reference comparison produces wrong significance verdicts.

title: rcicrely user guide

what: rcicrely — reliability assessment for reverse-correlation CIs
title: rcicrely — reliability assessment for reverse-correlation CIs
text: Reliability assessment reverse-correlation classification images. Within-condition consistency + -condition discriminability, without second-phase trait-rating study. rcicrely assesses reliability classification images (CIs) produced reverse-correlation experiments. answers questions infoVal doesn’t: signal CI stable, distinguishable comparison condition? Within-condition: permuted split-half Spearman–Brown correction, leave-one-sensitivity, ICC(3,1) ICC(3,k) via direct mean-squares. -condition: vectorised Welch pixel t, cluster-based permutation max-statistic FWER control, bootstrap representational dissimilarity. Supports standard 2IFC (via canonical rcicr) Brief-RC 12 (Schmitz, Rougier & Yzerbyt, 2024 — implemented natively). Companion rcicrdiagnostics: diagnostics catches silent data-processing errors CI computation; rcicrely asks whether CIs reliable discriminable cleanly computed.

code: library(rcicrely)  # Option A: from a directory of pre-computed CI images signal_A <- load_signal_matrix(   dir             = "data/cis_condition_A/",   base_image_path = "data/base.jpg" ) signal_B <- load_signal_matrix("data/cis_condition_B/", "data/base.jpg")  # Option B: from raw trial-level responses (2IFC) res_A <- ci_from_responses_2ifc(   responses  = my_responses_A,   rdata_path = "data/rcicr_stimuli.Rdata",   baseimage  = "base" ) signal_A <- res_A$signal_matrix  # Reliability reports within  <- run_within(signal_A, n_permutations = 2000, seed = 1L) between <- run_between(signal_A, signal_B,                        n_permutations = 2000, n_boot = 2000, seed = 1L)  print(within);  plot(within) print(between); plot(between)

what: Cluster-based permutation test with max-statistic FWER control — rel_cluster_test
title: Cluster-based permutation test with max-statistic FWER control — rel_cluster_test
text: Pixel-level discriminability test two condition signal matrices. Use identify image two conditions' CIs differ, family-wise error control across pixels. Pair rel_dissimilarity() overall magnitude. Implements Maris & Oostenveld (2007) approach Nichols & Holmes (2002) max-statistic family-wise error control: Compute observed pixel-wise Welch t. Threshold |t| > cluster_threshold (separately positive negative tails); label connected components 4-connectivity. observed cluster, cluster mass = sum t-values cluster (pixel count). Build null distribution via n_permutations stratified label permutations (preserves (N_A, N_B)). , find clusters record max positive mass max negative mass. Observed cluster's p-value = fraction null max-masses (matching sign) equal exceed observed mass absolute value. Stratification (sec.7.5): unstratified permutation lets two group sizes drift, changes Welch degrees freedom null biases max-mass distribution. preserve (N_A, N_B) exactly.

title: Leave-one-out sensitivity — rel_loo
text: Two outlier rules available: "sd" (default): flag producers r_loo < mean(r) - flag_threshold * sd(r). Standard convention; sensitive outliers trying detect. "mad": flag producers r_loo < median(r) - flag_threshold * mad(r). Robust atypical producers often show RC datasets skewed correlation distributions. default flag_threshold = 2.5 calibrated 30-producer dataset flags roughly 0.3 producers chance "sd", rather ~1.5 2-SD rule produce. "mad" roughly comparable thanks MAD's 1.4826 consistency factor.

title: Permuted split-half reliability with Spearman-Brown correction — rel_split_half
text: Measures stable condition's group-level CI across random halves producer set. n_permutations iterations: randomly partition producers (columns signal_matrix) two roughly-equal halves, average half get two group vectors, compute Pearson correlation. Use answer "get group CI run different half participants?". odd N, one randomly-chosen producer dropped per permutation (re-drawn iteration, fixed) halves floor(N/2) producers. Reports mean per-permutation r Spearman-Brown corrected reliability r_SB = (2 r) / (1 + r). 95% CI 2.5 / 97.5 percentile permutation distribution.

what: Raw vs. rendered CIs - documentation overhaul + safety net
title: rcicrely 0.1.1 (unreleased)
text: Reliability metrics operate raw mask (participant’s unscaled noise contribution). Reading PNGs disk via read_cis() silently loaded rendered CI (base + scaling(mask)) fed scaling(mask) downstream metrics, can distort variance-based statistics (rel_icc(), Euclidean dissimilarity, pixel_t_test(), rel_cluster_test()). canonical 2IFC infoVal path (rcicr::computeInfoVal2IFC()) unaffected extracts raw $ci element rcicr CI-list internally; hand-rolled infoVal implementations (Brief-RC, custom code) affected need raw mask. mathematical core package always correct; release sharpens boundary users see issue. read_cis(), extract_signal(), load_signal_matrix() now emit -per-session cli warning explaining raw-vs-rendered distinction, metrics affected, silence (acknowledge_scaling = TRUE argument options(rcicrely.silence_scaling_warning = TRUE)). Every rel_*() run_*() function now runs cheap heuristic entry (looks_scaled()) emits -per-session warning input matrix’s dynamic range looks rendered rather raw. Heuristic ; silencing options. vignette("tutorial") restructured function--function user guide (~600 lines, ~350). Adds dedicated “Mental model: signal matrix” chapter numerical demonstration scaling distorts variance-based metrics leaving Pearson correlation intact, “Interpreting results” chapter flags infoVal pitfall. README.md adds top-level “Important: raw vs. rendered CIs” callout.

title: rcicrely 0.1.1 (unreleased)

Mirrors ci_from_responses_2ifc()’s new keep_rendered = TRUE argument, adds $rendered_ci field extracted rcicr’s $combined.

title: rcicrely 0.1.1 (unreleased)
text: New flag_method = c("sd", "mad") argument (default "sd", preserves previous behavior). "mad" flags producers whose LOO correlation falls median(r) - flag_threshold * mad(r), robust atypical producers often dominate SD rule. flag_threshold_sd argument renamed flag_threshold (multiplier applies either SD MAD). old name still works alias existing code break. Result object gains $median_r, $mad_r, $flag_method, $flag_threshold fields.

Roxygen overhaul: every exported function now uniform @description, @section Reading result:, @section Common mistakes: blocks consistent help-page UX.

title: rcicrely 0.1.0
text: Initial public release.

title: rcicrely 0.1.0
text: rel_split_half() - permuted split-half Spearman-Brown correction; handles odd N via per-permutation drop. rel_loo() - leave-one-sensitivity 2.5-SD outlier flagging default. rel_icc() - ICC(3,1) ICC(3,k) via direct mean-squares (two-way mixed model: pixels fixed, participants random); ICC(2,*) available request. pixel_t_test() - vectorised Welch’s t per pixel. rel_cluster_test() - cluster-based permutation test 4-connectivity labelling max-statistic FWER control; permutation stratified (preserves N_A, N_B). rel_dissimilarity() - bootstrap correlation + Euclidean distance percentile CIs (boot dependency). run_within(), run_between() - run every metric, wrap rcicrely_report. read_cis(), extract_signal(), load_signal_matrix() pre-computed CI images. read_noise_matrix() rcicr .Rdata plain-text noise matrices. ci_from_responses_2ifc() - wraps rcicr::batchGenerateCI2IFC() known gotchas handled. ci_from_responses_briefrc() - native Brief-RC 12 implementation per Schmitz, Rougier & Yzerbyt (2024), matching genMask() exactly including duplicate-stimulus tie-breaking. S3 methods - print, summary, plot every rcicrely_* result class plus rcicrely_report.
